title: The Dialogue of Pessimism
description: The most nihilistic text from the ancient world — a master and slave agree with every proposal and its opposite.
introduction: The most nihilistic text from the ancient world — a master and slave agree with every proposal and its opposite.

translation.text: 'Slave, I will do a good deed.' 'Do, sir.' 'No, I will not.' 'Do not, sir. Go to the ancient ruins. See the skulls of the great and the lowly. Which is the benefactor?'
